data.label: Producer (s3 object store)
data.description: Opens a Data/Out channel `blocks` whose declared transport is `s3` — a DURABLE object store (rustfs/S3/GCS/Azure via OpenDAL), a fundamentally different transport SHAPE from the NATS pub/sub adapters (key/value, no subscribe primitive). It writes 8 binary blocks of 8 KiB each (64 KiB total) over the out-of-band transport; each element becomes one object at `…/blocks/c{seq}`. The compiler bakes `transport: s3` into the manifest; the SDK stamps it into the channel's `open` descriptor; the producer's executor dispatches the object-store PUT adapter off it. Zero SDK change — the SAME `open_output(...).write(...)` as the JetStream and nats-latest demos.

data.label: Consumer (unchanged stream())
data.description: Declares a Data/In channel `blocks` wired from the producer's open handle and drains it via the ORDINARY `for block in aithericon.stream('blocks')` API — unaware the bytes rode an object store instead of a NATS stream. The consumer's executor lifts `transport` from the producer's `open` descriptor and dispatches the matching object-store subscribe adapter (it polls the chunk objects in `seq` order). This proves the dispatch port is store-AGNOSTIC, not just NATS-flavour-agnostic. Unlike lossy nats-latest, S3 is durable + ordered + fully replayable: `received` MUST equal `sent` and `total_bytes` MUST equal `bytes_sent` — completeness, not currency.
